D10 Imaging L4 Analyze Reference

Film Grain vs Digital Noise

grain is silver crystals — clumped, scene-dependent, size scales with sensitivity; noise is per-pixel and grid-aligned, independent of neighbours

Film grain is the visual footprint of silver halide crystals developed into opaque silver — crystals are irregular in size, clump in dense regions, and a high-ISO film uses physically larger crystals. Digital noise is added per pixel on a regular grid — Gaussian or Poisson, independent across neighbours, no spatial clumping.

Two equal-exposure patches: left is rendered with random-position silver crystals (sizes drawn from the chosen film stock), right with per-pixel Gaussian noise. Drag ISO/sensitivity to scale both. Notice that grain has a chunky organic texture and noise has a salt-and-pepper grid look.
